posting.sponsorName: Joint Apprenticeship and Training Committee for the New York City District Council of Carpenters
posting.unionName: New York City District Council of Carpenters
posting.applicationSummary: Attend the required in-person information session at the NYCDCC Training Center, then use the passcode provided to complete and submit the online application within 60 days of the session.
posting.applicationInPersonAddress: NYCDCC Training Center, 395 Hudson Street (Clarkson Street entrance), 2nd floor, New York, NY 10014
posting.applicationHours: Information sessions are held the second Wednesday of each month from 9:00 AM to 10:00 AM during the recruitment period, except December and January. No admittance after 9:00 AM.
posting.applicationLimitDetails: Spaces are limited and filled on a first-come, first-served basis.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 17 years old at the time of application.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED.
posting.transportationRequirement: Must have reliable transportation to and from various job sites and classes at the approved school, and must travel to off-site training if required.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a substance abuse screening within 48 hours of selection and prior to enrollment, at the sponsor’s expense, and must agree to pre-enrollment, random, for-cause, and post-accident drug testing.
posting.classRequirement: Must travel to off-site training and attend classes at the approved school.
allRequirements: Must be at least 17 years old at the time of application.
Must have a high school diploma or a high school equivalency diploma such as TASC or GED. Proof will be required after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must be physically able to perform the work of the trade without posing a direct threat to the safety of themselves or others.

Must be physically able to perform the work of a Carpenter, which may include working on scaffolds and in confined spaces; working under inclement weather such as rain, snow, cold, heat, and direct sunlight; performing constant repetitive motions; climbing and working from heights; lifting items weighing a minimum of 50 pounds; and standing and stooping for prolonged periods of time.
Must pass a substance abuse screening, at the sponsor’s expense, within 48 hours of selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship, at a designated collection site designated by the JATC.
Must agree to and sign the Apprentice drug testing policy, which provides for pre-enrollment testing, random testing, for-cause testing, and post-accident testing.
Must agree to sign the Apprentice handbook, which is subject to change at any time.

Must travel to off-site training and agree to be financially responsible for certain costs related to such training if the apprentice cancels, fails to show up, or fails to successfully complete the program.
Must be able to read, hear, and understand verbal instructions and warnings in English.
Must provide proof, with detailed examples, of any trade-related experience, certification, or education after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must be a U.S. citizen or have the legal right to work in the United States. Proof will be required after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must have reliable means of transportation to and from various job sites and classes at the approved school.
Must provide DD-214, Certificate of Release or Discharge from Active Duty, if applicable, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 17, have a high school diploma or equivalency, be physically able to do carpentry work, have reliable transportation, meet English and work-authorization requirements, and pass required substance abuse testing.
posting.contactName: New York City District Council of Carpenters
posting.contactSummary: Contact the New York City District Council of Carpenters for further information at (212) 727-2224.
